meaning: n.<法> 法国雅各宾派,雅各宾党人adj.雅各宾派及其政策的n.[动] 毛领鸽
examples: Most states are Jacobin in spirit.
The logic was Jacobin, the authority deriving from a perceived mandate to recast time-honored practices.
The minimum singular value of the power flow Jacobin matrix can be used to evaluate the voltage stability margin.
Did he feel himself trapped into co-operating as a means of proving his anti-Jacobin credentials to a suspicious and conservative establishment?
The same Jacques-Louis David whose The Death of Marat lionized the Jacobin ideal of the French Revolution went on to glorify imperial rule in The Coronation of Napoleon.
